preguntas.pregunta: [1/10] When a machine or tool is made, the most suitable material must be chosen by considering its properties (mechanical, thermal, electrical and chemical). Q: How can properties be classified?
opciones: As metal, polymer, ceramic and composite.
As mechanical, thermal, electrical and chemical.
As soft, pasty, resistant and brittle.

preguntas.pregunta: [2/10] The main types of materials used in mechanical engineering are metals, polymer materials, ceramics and composite materials. Q: Which is NOT a main type of material?
[3/10] The most commonly used materials are metals, divided into ferrous and non-ferrous. They can be used pure or mixed with other elements. Q: What are the two forms to use metals?
opciones: In their pure form or mixed with other elements.

preguntas.pregunta: [4/10] In this second case we have an alloy and it is used to improve some properties of metals. Ferrous metals are iron and alloys using iron. Q: Why do engineers use an alloy?
opciones: To improve some properties of the metals.
To make metals softer and pasty.
To remove iron from the mixture.

preguntas.pregunta: [5/10] Because iron is soft and pasty it is not suitable as a structural material, so a small amount of carbon is added to make steel alloy. Q: What is added to iron to create steel?

[6/10] Non-ferrous metals contain little or no iron. The most common non-ferrous metals used in mechanics are copper, zinc, tin and aluminium. Q: What is the main characteristic of non-ferrous metals?
opciones: They contain little or no iron.
They are composed mostly of carbon.
They are exclusively used in buildings.

preguntas.pregunta: [7/10] Common non-ferrous alloys are brass (copper and zinc), bronze (copper and tin) and aluminium alloys used in the aircraft industry. Q: Which industry uses aluminium alloys?
[8/10] Other examples of materials are plastic and rubber. PVC or polyvinyl chloride is a type of plastic used to insulate wires and cables. Q: What is PVC used for?
opciones: To mix with copper and create brass.

preguntas.pregunta: [9/10] Rubber is a polymer and its best property is elasticity (returns to its original shape). Ceramics are insulators: hard, strong, but brittle. Q: What happens to rubber after deformation?
opciones: It breaks immediately because it is brittle.
It returns to its original size and shape.
It permanently changes its electrical properties.

preguntas.pregunta: [10/10] Composite materials are two or more materials combined to improve properties. Concrete is reinforced with steel and used in building. Q: In building, what is concrete reinforced with?
